  1. Home
  2. rule
  3. Europe
  4. Germany

Germany

How to Distinguish

  • The country-code domain is .de
  • Einbahnstrasse marks a one-way street
  • German uses the Eszett character ß
  • Bollards carry white or orange reflectors
  • Bus and tram stops display a green H (Haltestelle) symbol
  • Company names often include GmbH on signs
  • Legacy coverage featured square blur and mostly Gen2 imagery(by neckoluv ); new Street View refreshes began rolling out after July 2023(References Google to update Street View in Germany this year)
  • Some town signs include QR codes that link to local information
  • Urban areas rarely show exposed utility poles(References ??????????????????????????????????????)

Signs you can find

Representative Companies

    An Einbahnstrasse sign means you are in Germany, while the shorter EINBAHN indicates Austria. No-parking zones allow short stops and use arrowed signs. Watch for the Eszett ß in German text.

    Orange background signs with arrows and road numbers are widespread. White signs retain black outlines. Comparing signage styles helps identify bridge border crossings.

    Bollards typically have white bodies with orange or white reflectors. Look for forested roads with tidy shoulders.

    Bus and tram stops bear green “H” circles marked Haltestelle.

    Older Gen2 imagery produces boxy blur and muted colors. Newer updates offer sharper imagery in major cities.

    Look for the green pharmacy sign marked Apotheke(References Apotheke).

    Deutsche Post and DHL branding is widespread; DHL is a major global logistics group(References DHL Group).

    Aral gas stations are common across Germany(References Aral AG).

    Germany saw a Street View refresh in 2023. When you see a 2023 Google copyright watermark, the coverage is newer and often clearer than the older Gen2 imagery.

    Autobahn junction plates use yellow shielding with A### numbering. Local roads carry B, L, or K prefixes.

    {{< amazoncard url=“https://amzn.to/3Kscu3P” title=“67 Ways to Know Germany, 3rd Edition (Area Studies)” price=“JPY 2,200” tagline=“A concise Germany area-studies guide covering history, society, economy, and culture in short chapters, updated for recent years.”

    }}

    Narrow down state/region

    • Brick villages with orange tiles are more common in the north
    • Large prefabricated housing blocks hint at former East Germany
    • Central Germany can show small-town landscapes and gentle hills
    • Slate roofs cluster around the Trier/Burgen area(References Fell Exhibition Slate Mine)
    • Fachhallenhaus-style farmhouses appear in northern Germany near the Netherlands(References Low German house)
    • The German Timber-Frame Road links traditional half-timbered towns(References German Timber-Frame Road)
      • North
      • South

      Example village: Stellichte.

      Example town: Hoyerswerda(References Hoyerswerda).

      Example town: Burglauer.

      Slate roofs are common around Trier and Burgen, close to the Fell Exhibition Slate Mine(References Fell Exhibition Slate Mine).

      By P170 - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

      Northern Germany has Fachhallenhaus farmhouses, sometimes resembling Belgium or Dutch styles.

      This route highlights historic half-timbered towns and preservation districts.

      By Deutsche Fachwerkstrasse - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

      • German place-name suffixes can hint at region(References Suffixes of German towns and villages)
      • North-south differences show in climate and landscape
      • Telephone area codes provide rough regional hints
      • Industrial density clusters in specific regions

        Use suffix maps to estimate region quickly.

        German name place endings (e.g., \"-burg\" vs \"-dorf\" vs \"-inghausen\", etc.) [1536x1572] [x-post /r/DataArt]
        byu/jmerlinb inMapPorn

        By Botaurus-stellaris - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0, Link

        By Maximilian Dorrbecker (Chumwa) - Own work, CC BY-SA 2.5, Link

        Forests & terrain

        • The Harz and Thuringian Forest have dense spruce corridors
        • North Sea islands such as Sylt contain treeless dunes
        • Rape fields bloom bright yellow each spring

          Expect evergreen forests, logging clearings, and mossy shoulders.

          Sylt contains dune landscapes devoid of trees(References Sylt).

          In spring, bright-yellow rapeseed fields dominate the countryside.

          Cities

          • Hamburg features historic brick warehouses along canals
          • Each major city uses different street-name plate styles(by neckoluv )

            Brick warehouses line the canals(References Hamburg).

            • Berlin: white plates with gray borders attached by metal straps
            • Munich: blue plates with indented corners and raised dots
            • Cologne: simple white plates
            • More examples: A GeoGuessr guide to Germany (by Steve)

            Many intersections include historical street plates and district info.

            Industrial density varies: GfK maps highlight clusters of manufacturing employees per 1,000 inhabitants(References Map of the month: Industrial density, Germany. Illustration: GfK).

            Illustration: GfK, Link

            Original image source

            • Right pole photo credit: By Ralf Krebs - Own work, CC BY 3.0, Link

            {{< amazon-links >}}

            Representative companies

            Company Ticker Overview IR Dividends
            BASF - Germany's largest chemical company; its name comes from Badische Anilin- und Sodafabrik. -
            Hapag-Lloyd - One of the world's major container shipping lines, headquartered in Germany. -
